---
jupyter: python3
format: 
  pdf:
    echo: false
    geometry:
      - top=10mm        
      - left=10mm
      - right=10mm
      - bottom=20mm
      - headheight=40mm 
      - headsep=5mm 
      - includehead=true 
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          \usepackage{tabularx}
          \usepackage{multirow}
          \usepackage{graphicx}
          \usepackage{xcolor}
          \usepackage{fancyhdr}
          \usepackage{lastpage}
          
          % Define the Page Style
          \pagestyle{fancy}
          \fancyhf{} 
          \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt}
          
          % Define the Header Content
          \fancyhead[C]{
            \noindent
            \bgroup
            \def\arraystretch{1.4}
            \begin{tabularx}{\textwidth}{|p{5.5cm}|p{1.8cm}|X|p{1.2cm}|p{1.8cm}|}
            \hline
            \multirow{5}{5.5cm}{
                \vspace*{1mm}
                \includegraphics[width=4.5cm]{logo} \\
                \fontsize{6pt}{7pt}\selectfont
                \textbf{CIVIL \& ENVIRONMENTAL ENGINEERING,}\\
                SKEMPTON BUILDING, IMPERIAL COLLEGE\\
                LONDON, SOUTH KENSINGTON CAMPUS,\\
                LONDON SW7 2AZ.\\[3mm]
                \normalsize\textbf{CALCULATION SHEET}
            }
            & \textbf{Job No.} & & \textbf{Sheet} & \textbf{Ref} \\ \cline{2-5}
            & \textbf{Job Title} & Steel Design Coursework & \multicolumn{2}{l|}{\thepage\ of \pageref{LastPage}} \\ \cline{2-5}
            & \multicolumn{4}{p{\dimexpr\textwidth-5.5cm-8\tabcolsep-5\arrayrulewidth\relax}|}{
                \textbf{Subject} \newline
                \vspace*{1mm}
                \centerline{NS-Truss Sizing and Connection Calculations (Scheme 1)}
                \vspace*{1mm}
            } \\ \cline{2-5}
            & \multirow{2}{*}{\textbf{Client}} & \textbf{Made by} \quad JT MA & \multicolumn{2}{l|}{\textbf{Date} \quad 29/03/2023} \\ \cline{3-5}
            & & \textbf{Checked by} & \multicolumn{2}{l|}{\textbf{Date}} \\ \hline
            \end{tabularx}
            \egroup
          }
---
#

```{python}
#material
fu= 500.0
fy = 355.0
E = 210*10**3

import pandas as pd
df_all = pd.read_csv('section_mm.csv')
if df_all.columns[0] == 'Unnamed: 0':
  df_all = df_all.rename(columns = {'Unnamed: 0' : 'Section Profile'})

section = '356 x 406x 634'
df = df_all[df_all['Section Profile'] == section]
#section
h = float(df['h (mm)'].iloc[0])
b = float(df['b (mm)'].iloc[0])
tw = float(df['tw (mm)'].iloc[0])
tf = float(df['tf (mm)'].iloc[0])
d = float(df['d (mm)'].iloc[0])
r= float(df['r(mm)'].iloc[0])
A = float(df['A (mm^2)'].iloc[0])
Iy = float(df['Iy (mm^4)'].iloc[0])
Iz = float(df['Iz (mm^4)'].iloc[0])
Ry = float(df['R_y (mm)'].iloc[0])
Rz = float(df['R_z (mm)'].iloc[0])
Ey = float(df['W_el,y (mm^3)'].iloc[0])
Ez = float(df['W_el,z (mm^3)'].iloc[0])
Py = float(df['W_pl,y (mm3)'].iloc[0])
Pz = float(df['W,pl,z (mm^3)'].iloc[0])
Iw = float(df['I_w (mm^6)'].iloc[0])
It = float(df['I_T (mm^4)'].iloc[0])
```

## Effective length of column about y-y

\begin{description}
  \item[Ultimate strength:] $f_{u}$ = `{python} fy` $N\cdot mm^{-2}$  
  \item[Yield strength:] $f_{y}$ = `{python} fu` $N \cdot mm^{-2}$  
  \item[Young modulus:] E = `{python} E` $N\cdot mm^{-2}$  
\end{description}

## Section Properties

\begin{description}
  \item[Overall depth, h] = `{python} h` mm  
  \item[Width of section, b] = `{python} b` mm  
  \item[Web thickness, $t_{w}$] = `{python} tw`  mm  
  \item[Flange thickness, $t_{f}$] = `{python} tf` mm  
  \item[De pth between fillet, $h_{w}$] = `{python} d` mm  
  \item[Root radius, r] = `{python} r` mm  
  \item[Area of section, $A$] = `{python} A`  $mm^{2}$   
  \item[2nd moment area Major axis, $I_{y}$] = `{python} Iy` $mm^{4}$  
  \item[2nd moment area minor axis, $I_{z}$] = `{python} Iz` $mm^{4}$  
  \item[Radius of gyration major axis $i_{y}$] = `{python} Ry` mm  
  \item[Radius of gyration minor axis, $i_{z}$] = `{python} Rz` mm  
  \item[Elastic modulus major axis, $W_{el,y}$] = `{python} Ey` $mm^{3}$  
  \item[Elastic modulus minor axis, $W_{el,z}$] = `{python} Ez` $mm^{3}$  
  \item[Plastic modulus major axis, $W_{pl,y}$] = `{python} Py` $mm^{3}$  
  \item[Plastic modulus minor axis, $W_{pl,z}$] = `{python} Pz` $mm^{3}$  
  \item[Warping Constant, $I_{w}$] = `{python} Iw` $mm^{6}$  
  \item[Torsional constant, $I_{t}$] = `{python} It` $mm^{4}$  
\end{description}

```{python}
import math
def rd (number):
  number = round(number,4)
  return number
ep = math.sqrt(235/fy)
crf = rd(9*ep)
crw = rd(33*ep)
cw = d
c1 = rd(cw/tw)
cf = rd((b-tw-2*r)/2)
c2 = rd(cf/tf)
```

## Section Classification

\begin{description}
  \item[$\epsilon$] = $\sqrt{\frac{235}{f_{y}}}$ = `{python} ep` \\[2ex]  
  \item[$c_{w}$] = $\frac{(b-t_{w}-2\cdot r)}{2}$ = `{python} cw` \\[2ex]  
  \item[Web class number] = $\frac{c}{t_{f}}$ = `{python} c1`  \\[2ex]   
  \item[$c_{f}$] = Depth between filet = `{python cf}`  \\[2ex]  
  \item[Flange class number] = $\frac{c_{star}}{t_{w}}$ = `{python} c2`  \\[2ex]
\end{description}
  Since `{python} c1` < `{python} crw` and `{python} c2` < `{python} crf`, the section belongs to class 1  

```{python}
def buckle (Lcr, I, E, A, fy, a, h, b):
  Lcr = 6
  Ncr = (math.pi**2 * E * I) / (Lcr**2)
  lam = math.sqrt(( A*fy / Ncr ))
  ratio = h/b
  if ratio <= 1.2:
    phi = 0.5*(1 + a*(lam - 0.2) + lam**2)
    chi = 1/(phi + math.sqrt(phi**2 - lam**2))
  return Ncr, lam, ratio, phi, chi

Lcry = 3
ay = 0.34
Ncry, lamy, ratio, phiy, chiy = buckle(Lcry, Iy, E, A, fy, ay, h, b)

Lcrz = 3
az = 0.49
Ncrz, lamz, ratio, phiz, chiz = buckle(Lcrz, Iz, E, A, fy, az, h, b)

chi = min([chiy,chiz])
Nrd = chi*A*fy
```
## Buckling resistance 

\begin{description}
  \item[$L_{cr,y}$] = $\frac{6}{2}$ = `{python} Lcry`
  \item[$N_{cr,y}$] = $\frac{(\pi^{2}\cdot E\cdot I_{y})}{{L_{cr,y}}^{2}}$ = `{python} Ncry` kN
  \item[$\lambda_{y}$] = $\sqrt{\frac{(A\cdot f_{y})}{N_{cr,y}}}$ = `{python} lamy`
  \item[$\frac{h}{b}$] = `{python} ratio` < 1.2
  \item[$t_{f}$] = `{python} tf` mm < 100~mm
\end{description}
Use buckling curve B,  $\alpha_{y}$ = `{python} ay`
\begine{description}
  \item[$\phi_{y}$] = $0.5 \cdot (1 + \alpha_{y} \cdot (\lambda_{y} - 0.2) + {\lambda_{y}}^{2})$ = `{python} phiy`
  \item[$\chi_{y}$] = $\frac{1}{\phi_{y} + \sqrt{{\phi_{y}}^{2} - {\lambda_{y}}^{2}}}$ = `{python} chiy`
\end{description}

\begin{description}
  \item[$L_{cr,z}$] = $\frac{6}{2}$ = `{python} Lcrz`
  \item[$N_{cr,z}$] = $\frac{(\pi^{2}\cdot E\cdot I_{z})}{{L_{cr,z}}^{2}}$ = `{python} Ncrz` kN
  \item[$\lambda_{z}$] = $\sqrt{\frac{(A\cdot f_{y})}{N_{cr,z}}}$ = `{python} lamz`
  \item[$\frac{h}{b}$] = `{python} ratio` < 1.2
  \item[$t_{f}$] = `{python} tf` mm < 100~mm
\end{description}
Use buckling curve C,  $\alpha_{z}$ = `{python} az`
\begine{description}
  \item[$\phi_{z}$] = $0.5 \cdot (1 + \alpha_{z} \cdot (\lambda_{z} - 0.2) + {\lambda_{z}}^{2})$ = `{python} phiz`
  \item[$\chi_{z}$] = $\frac{1}{\phi_{z} + \sqrt{{\phi_{z}}^{2} - {\lambda_{z}}^{2}}}$ = `{python} chiz`
\end{description}

{{<pagebreak>}}

#

\begine{description}
  \item[$\chi$] = min($\chi_{y},\chi_{z}$) = `{python} chi}`
  \item[$N_{RD\_634\_6m}$] = $\chi\cdot A\cdot f_{y}$ = `{python} Nrd`
\end{description}
